content.body:
Creates the minimum project context needed to open Claude Code directly
in copilot-core and work on the system itself rather than as a
side-effect of downstream project sessions.

Files:
- .claude/project-config.yml — name, stack, locales, self_hosting: true
- .claude/context/project.md — what copilot-core is, how it evolves
- .claude/context/self-hosting-notes.md — propagation-is-instant warnings,
  Leo-editing-Leo lag, cross-project Manager inheritance, language
  inconsistency flag, known loose ends

The scaffold is intentionally minimal — no project-specific Managers,
no rule overrides, no specialists. Core Managers and rules already
apply via the global sync.sh symlinks.

Motivation: today's Logbook session produced a core commit (d8bdb6e,
conventions doc) as a side-effect. Founder flagged context-switching
cost and asked to separate core evolution from project work. Opening
Leo directly in the core unlocks that separation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
content.subject: chore: scaffold .claude/ for self-hosting sessions
content.summary:
scaffold .claude/ for self-hosting sessions. Creates the minimum project context needed to open Claude Code directly
